growth_records.strain: Enrichment from distillery-wastewater UASB (Biothane, Delft) + potato-starch acidification-tank sludge (Smiths Food)

up.method: Specific substrate uptake rate -qs (slope of substrate vs time / active biomass); Table 2 continuous operation

sec.method: calculated = yield 0.18 Cmol/Cmol S x qs 0.89
calculated = yield 0.13 mol/Cmol S x qs 0.89

growth_records.cite: Temudo, Margarida F. et al. Xylose anaerobic conversion by open-mixed cultures. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2009). DOI:10.1007/s00253-008-1749-y. [PMC7419444]
growth_records.snippet: Xylose 0.133 Cmol/L; Dilution rate 0.128 h-1; Biomass yield 0.16 Cmol/Cmol; Acetate 0.18, Butyrate 0.46, Ethanol 0.017, Formate 0.20 (Cmol/Cmol S); H2 0.13 mol/Cmol S. Table 2: -qs = 0.89 Cmol/Cmol X h.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state so growth_rate = dilution rate D = 0.128 h-1 (Table 1). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.128 = 5.42 h. Substrate uptake qs=0.89 experimental (Table 2). Product secretion rates derived as product_yield(Cmol/Cmol S, Table 1) x qs(0.89): acetate 0.18x0.89=0.160; butyrate 0.46x0.89=0.409; ethanol 0.017x0.89=0.0151; formate 0.20x0.89=0.178; H2 0.13x0.89=0.116 mol/Cmol X h. Succinate & propionate <0.02 Cmol/Cmol (below reporting). Carbon/electron recovery 100+/-10%. Mixed (undefined) prokaryotic community, not a single strain.

growth_records.strain: Enrichment from distillery-wastewater UASB + potato-starch acidification sludge

up.method: Specific substrate uptake rate -qs; Table 2 continuous operation

sec.method: calculated = yield 0.05 mol/Cmol S x qs 0.62

growth_records.cite: Temudo, Margarida F. et al. Xylose anaerobic conversion by open-mixed cultures. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2009). DOI:10.1007/s00253-008-1749-y. [PMC7419444]
growth_records.snippet: Glucose 0.133 Cmol/L; Dilution rate 0.128 h-1; Biomass yield 0.21 Cmol/Cmol; Acetate 0.16, Butyrate 0.30, Ethanol 0.08, Formate 0.22 (Cmol/Cmol S); H2 0.05 mol/Cmol S. Table 2: -qs = 0.62 Cmol/Cmol X h.
growth_records.notes: Same reactor/conditions as xylose but glucose feed (Table 1 middle column). mu = D = 0.128 h-1; td=0.693/0.128=5.42 h. qs=0.62 experimental (Table 2). Secretion = yield x qs: acetate 0.16x0.62=0.0992; butyrate 0.30x0.62=0.186; ethanol 0.08x0.62=0.0496; formate 0.22x0.62=0.136; H2 0.05x0.62=0.031 mol/Cmol X h.

growth_records.strain: Enrichment from distillery-wastewater UASB + potato-starch acidification sludge

sec.method: product yield (Table 1); specific qs not reported for 0.333 Cmol/L run

growth_records.cite: Temudo, Margarida F. et al. Xylose anaerobic conversion by open-mixed cultures. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2009). DOI:10.1007/s00253-008-1749-y. [PMC7419444]
growth_records.snippet: Xylose 0.333 Cmol/L; Dilution rate 0.134 h-1; Biomass yield 0.13 Cmol/Cmol; product spectrum shifted to more ethanol (0.23) and acetate (0.22) at expense of butyrate (0.07). Formate 0.18, H2 0.06 mol/Cmol S.
growth_records.notes: Higher-concentration xylose chemostat (Table 1 right column). mu = D = 0.134 h-1; td=0.693/0.134=5.17 h. Specific substrate uptake -qs NOT reported for this run (Table 2 only lists 0.133 continuous), so uptake_rates empty; product yields reported as Cmol/Cmol S (experimental). Product-yield shift vs 0.133 run documents concentration-dependent metabolic shift.

growth_records.medium: Xylose pulse (1st addition) into xylose-fed chemostat

up.method: Initial specific uptake rate -qs during pulse (Table 2)

growth_records.cite: Temudo, Margarida F. et al. Xylose anaerobic conversion by open-mixed cultures. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2009). DOI:10.1007/s00253-008-1749-y. [PMC7419444]
growth_records.snippet: Pulse experiments, Xylose 1st addition 0.067 Cmol/L: -qs = 1.01 Cmol/Cmol X L h; Biomass yield 0.081 Cmol/Cmol (Table 2).
growth_records.notes: Substrate non-limiting pulse in chemostat; growth rate/mu not reported for transient pulse (rates set by substrate not dilution). Catabolic products same as continuous (mainly butyrate + acetate) but per-pulse yields not tabulated, so secretion_rates empty. qs and biomass yield experimental (Table 2).

growth_records.medium: Xylose pulse (2nd addition) into xylose-fed chemostat
growth_records.cite: Temudo, Margarida F. et al. Xylose anaerobic conversion by open-mixed cultures. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2009). DOI:10.1007/s00253-008-1749-y. [PMC7419444]
growth_records.snippet: Pulse experiments, Xylose 2nd addition 0.167 Cmol/L: -qs = 0.55 Cmol/Cmol X L h; Biomass yield 0.064 (Table 2). Uptake rate decreased 26% vs 1st pulse (substrate/product inhibition).
growth_records.notes: Higher-dose 2nd pulse; -qs dropped 26% (1.01 -> 0.55) suggesting substrate/product inhibition. mu not reported (transient). qs & yield experimental (Table 2).

up.method: Specific uptake rate -qs during glucose pulse (Table 2)

growth_records.cite: Temudo, Margarida F. et al. Xylose anaerobic conversion by open-mixed cultures. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2009). DOI:10.1007/s00253-008-1749-y. [PMC7419444]
growth_records.snippet: Pulse experiments, Glucose 0.083 Cmol/L: -qs = 0.78 Cmol/Cmol X L h; Biomass yield 0.143 (Table 2). Despite lower uptake, biomass yield higher on glucose (0.14) than xylose (0.08).
growth_records.notes: Xylose-grown community converts glucose immediately. mu not reported. qs & yield experimental (Table 2). Higher biomass yield on glucose attributed to substrate (ATP-costly active xylose uptake).

growth_records.medium: Xylose+glucose mixed pulse into xylose-fed chemostat
growth_records.cite: Temudo, Margarida F. et al. Xylose anaerobic conversion by open-mixed cultures. Applied Microbiology and Biotechnology (2009). DOI:10.1007/s00253-008-1749-y. [PMC7419444]
growth_records.snippet: Xylose and glucose pulse Phase 1: combined -qs 0.97, Xylose 0.56, Glucose 0.41 Cmol/Cmol X L h; combined biomass yield 0.111 (Table 2). Glucose uptake ~20% lower than xylose; both consumed simultaneously, no preference.
growth_records.notes: Co-substrate pulse; per-substrate biomass yields n.a. (only combined 0.111). Glucose qs (0.41) ~20% lower than xylose qs (0.56); combined 0.97 (Table 2). mu not reported (transient). All -qs experimental.
